title: ranunculus pentandrus var. platycarpus
intro: Ranunculus pentandrus var. platycarpus, commonly known as the platycarpus buttercup, is a variety of the Ranunculus pentandrus plant, a flowering perennial native to Western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. Its health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, due to its high content of alkaloids and flavonoids. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
